file_insights.changes: Added security headers middleware with 3 headers (x-content-type-options, x-frame-options, cache-control); applied to both production router and test router; added comprehensive test coverage
key_additions: test_security_headers unit test with assertions for all 3 headers

patterns_discovered.description: Used middleware::from_fn() to wrap a custom async function that modifies response headers; applied via .layer() method chaining
patterns_discovered.relevance: Established pattern for request/response interception in axum framework

patterns_discovered.description: Called response.headers_mut() to get mutable header reference, then used header::HeaderName and header::HeaderValue for type-safe header insertion
patterns_discovered.relevance: Ensures compile-time safety for header key-value pairs

patterns_discovered.description: Applied identical middleware layers to test router (make_app function) as production router, ensuring test routes have same behavior
patterns_discovered.relevance: Prevents test-production divergence and catches middleware bugs early

patterns_discovered.description: Single focused test verifies all 3 security headers with specific expected values and descriptive assertion messages
patterns_discovered.relevance: Guards against header removal or value mutation during future refactors

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Test router construction requires middleware application
gotchas_discovered.description: Initial implementation likely forgot to add .layer(middleware::from_fn()) to test router; diff shows it was included, preventing divergence between test and production routes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always mirror production middleware stack in test fixtures

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Header names are case-insensitive but must be lowercase for consistency
gotchas_discovered.description: Code uses header::HeaderName::from_static() for custom headers like 'x-content-type-options' (lowercase), while CACHE_CONTROL constant is used for standard header; header value retrieval via .get() is case-insensitive but standard form matters for client parsing
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use lowercase for custom headers; use axum's header:: constants for standard headers when available

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Middleware layer ordering matters for interception point
gotchas_discovered.description: Security headers middleware must wrap the route to intercept all responses; if placed after body limit layer, could miss certain error responses
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify middleware ordering; typically security headers should be outermost layer

completion_markers: Middleware function implemented with 3 security headers

recommendations.details: Add X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies, X-XSS-Protection, and Referrer-Policy for defense-in-depth; Strict-Transport-Security if HTTPS enforcement is required
Consider environment variables or feature flags to enable/disable specific security headers for compatibility with legacy clients if needed
Add comments explaining rationale for each header (nosniff prevents MIME sniffing, DENY blocks framing, no-store prevents caching)
Current test only checks successful 201 response; verify headers are present on error paths (4xx, 5xx) to ensure middleware catches all response types
